channel.title: Posts on My Portfolio
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on My Portfolio

item.title: Future
item.description:
This is soemthing that i want to tell my parents or having a mature mind!! idk.

as a young programmer from “India” theres a mentality i with you have to choose either engineer or doctor but in my case they force me !!

is a dead-fact i dont even choose cumputer as a subject so this lacks my certification over computers or programming language

as future for computing and ai in my point of veiw ai would not replace programming but make it fast and more user frendly.

item.title: Window Manager
item.description:
Hyprland and Sway

i like both of them and personaly i like hyprland not because of large community but for a good wayland compositor with in built blur and decoration.

and for sway i like it minimalism no heary dependencies ready in 20 to 30 mins

Hyprland vs. Sway: The Wayland Showdown

If you are moving away from X11, you are likely looking at these two powerhouses. Both are Tiling Window Managers (TWMs) built for Wayland, but they offer very different philosophies for your desktop experience.

item.title: Neovim and journey of coding
item.description:
Neovim

i use neovim for most for the time in my laptop. using archlinux with Noctalia shell, custome plugins and config

  • Sharingan.nvim

  • my neovim consist of

    • plugin managaer
    • theme {catppuccin-mocha}
    • my custome plugins {git,app-launcher,tangle,etc}

  • github plugins

    • my fav. snacks.nvim
    • and many more

just not becasue of plugins i like neovim i like becasue of speed and terminal based editor

my coding journey

when i was introduced by linux in 2020 it was hard for me to use

item.title: Welcome to My Portfolio
item.description:
Welcome

This is my first post on my Hugo portfolio website.

Getting Started

Here’s how you can set up your own Hugo site:

hugo new site mysite
cd mysite
git submodule add https://github.com/Livour/hugo-mana-theme.git themes/mana

Enjoy building with Hugo!
